mission.title: spire: Move to GitHub Actions
mission.description: Move to GitHub Actions. This issue affects 7+ users.

steps.title: Identify spire feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your spire deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=spire
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Move to GitHub Actions

steps.title: Check current Service configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant spire resources:
```bash
kubectl get service -A
kubectl describe service <name> -n <namespace>
```
Travis has begun charging the project lots of money to run CI/CD. I contacted the CNCF service desk to ask what we should do, and they indicated that we should move to GitHub Actions.

The project has

steps.title: Apply the fix for Move to GitHub Actions
steps.description:
This commit sets up GitHub Actions for SPIRE CI per CNCF request.

The nested rotation test is currently failing due to a problem with the docker workload attestor. The VMs that GitHub runs the Action jobs in ships with a docker that is set up to use custom cgroup names. This is causing the docker workload attestor to not recognize callers in docker containers. I think the fix will be to introduce a second agent config that has the correct matcher defined, and put a switch in the integration tes

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm Move to GitHub Actions is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: This commit sets up GitHub Actions for SPIRE CI per CNCF request.

The nested rotation test is currently failing due to a problem with the docker workload attestor. The VMs that GitHub runs the Action jobs in ships with a docker that is set up to use custom cgroup names. This is causing the docker workload attestor to not recognize callers in docker containers. I think the fix will be to introduce. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with spire installed or the issue environment reproducible.
